authors: A collection derived from the web community (Reddit,Pinterest,DmsGuild,ecc.). For credits, authors are provided with a web link in the entity description

language.name: Arachnos
entries: Arachnos is a fluid and fast spoken language, where the inflections put on syllables and words are very important to the meaning. These characteristics sometimes carry over when spidarans speak other languages, with them speaking too fast and using inflections in ways that may not sound right to others.

language.name: Doggish
entries: Doggish is a spoken language only, a series of barks and growls.

language.name: Aeramanu
entries: Aeramanu speech is punctuated with chirps, which they use to add meaning to their words much as humans use facial expressions. However, this can lead to miscommunication when other races fail to pick up on these cues.

language.name: Wuxian
entries: Wuxian is a strange tongue, one that tends to ignore traits of other languages, such as writing words as symbols instead of letters.

language.name: Pandaren
entries: Pandaren is an exotic-sounding language, similar to modern-day chinese.

language.name: Saurian
entries: The saurian dialect of Draconic is obvious to other speakers of the language and unless you learn Draconic again from another source, your speech in Draconic will always be notable for its saurian idiosyncrasies.

language.name: Merfolk Extended
entries: Merfolk is a language that can only be spoken while in water and is extremely difficult for non Mer to hear.

language.name: Dwarvish
entries: The Nezumi dialect is almost its own language, full of snapping and squeaking, yet it is written in Dwarven and there are enough words in common to make communication possible.
